slides.title: Advertising and Persuasion
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 8 English, Unit 4: Advertising and Persuasion. Welcome to Unit 4 of Year 8: Advertising and Persuasion! Every day, you are surrounded by messages designed to grab your attention, change your mind, or convince you to buy something. From billboards towering over busy Mogadishu streets to sponsored posts sliding through your social media feed, advertising is everywhere. But how does it work? What techniques do advertisers use to persuade people? And how can you, as a critical thinker, tell the difference between honest information and clever manipulation? In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand passages about advertising techniques, persuasion, and ethical marketing, and discuss their ideas confidently
Identify and use prepositions preceding nouns correctly in phrases like on sale, in stock, for a limited time, at a discount, and with interest
Form and use passive voice sentences in the present and past: "The product was launched by the company" and "The advert is designed to attract young...
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand passages about advertising techniques, persuasion, and ethical marketing, and discuss their ideas confidently. you will also identify and use prepositions preceding nouns correctly in phrases like on sale, in stock, for a limited time, at a discount, and with interest. you will also form and use passive voice sentences in the present and past: "the product was launched by the company" and "the advert is designed to attract young people.". Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: aptitude, adversary, cunning, momentum, bonanza, concession. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Advertising and Persuasion: source text 1 and Advertising and Persuasion: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Prepositions Preceding Nouns
bullets: What it means and why it matters Prepositions are words that show the relationship between a noun and other parts of a sentence. When we say "prepositions...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Prepositions Preceding Nouns. What it means and why it matters Prepositions are words that show the relationship between a noun and other parts of a sentence. When we say "prepositions preceding nouns," we mean prepositions that come before nouns to create common phrases. In advertising, these phrases appear constantly: items on sale, products in stock, offers valid for a limited time. Learning these fixed phrases will make your English sound natural and help you understand advertisements more easily. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
